Module 5:
Over-the-shoulder Real Life
Example - The Tennis Ninja
Launch
!
Executive Summary
We’re now going to go through Will Hamilton’s first piece of Pre-Launch
Content for his Tennis Ninja launch.
• 	Launch Details:
• 	17,000 person list
• 	$168,328 in sales
• 	Achieved this less than one year after becoming a PLF Owner
• 	Will’s launches have gradually increased from $35k to $65k to $105k
to $168k (this launch) to now $400k+
• 	One of the key benefits Will experienced is called the “Launch Echo”,
which is the inevitable positive effects of doing a launch. When he did
this launch it attracted the attention of the Bryan brothers - the most
successful doubles team in history.
• 	This led to partnering with them, which eventually led to more
opportunities with other tennis pro’s as well.
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.

-- 1 of 3 --

Video Overview
• 	The video starts by immediately delivering a big promise and shows
credibility
• 	Builds massive authority right away - he’s on the court with Roger
Federer behind him
• 	Foreshadows strategy course - this is completely up to you
• 	Flips some conventional wisdom (a good hook( and explains that
strategy is the key
• 	Continues to build massive authority into his PLC in a natural way
• 	Delivers a “behind the scenes” insight - massive authority
• 	Transitions into teaching in the studio - foreshadows the lessons and
video series
• 	Begins teaching session by foreshadowing that this is a video sequence
and you should watch it all
• 	Changes scenes and teaches in different modalities - definitely do this if
you can
• 	Promises a specific tactic for a specific problem - states the problem and
then quickly agitates it
• 	Ends with a CTA (call to action) - asks a specific question to viewers
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 3 --

• 	Specifically asks viewers to reflect on the lessons and whether they
are using them - this creates a “gap” in their mind
• 	One tip from Will’s video: In general you wouldn't want to wear
sunglasses - it’s important to show people your eyes
!
Key Lessons Learned
Always deliver that big promise first
The more authority you can build from the start the better - but do it in a
casual way
Foreshadowing the video sequence and course is key
State the problem and agitate - promise to solve the problem in next
video
!
